methodology_sections: 1. Lifecycle Integrity - State transitions, illegal states, predecessors
2. Monotonicity & Irreversibility - Increasing values, one-way transitions
3. Access & Permission Logic - Conditional access, role requirements
4. Time & Resource Integrity - Time-locks, resource bounds, expiry

invariants.description: onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses OR (||) instead of AND (&&), requiring the caller to be BOTH the vault maintainer AND have EMERGENCY_ROLE. This is inconsistent with onlyVaultGoverner and onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock which correctly use AND (&&). Functions gated by this modifier (addConnector, removeTrustedPosition, updateConnectorTrustedTokens) become inaccessible to the intended maintainer.
invariants.attack_scenario: The vault maintainer (a timelock contract) cannot add/remove connectors or update trusted tokens unless it also has the EMERGENCY_ROLE. This effectively bricks vault configuration by the maintainer alone. Only an address holding both roles simultaneously can call these functions, which is likely never the case in normal operation.
invariants.invariant_logic: onlyVaultMaintainer should allow (msg.sender == maintainer) OR hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender), currently requires BOTH
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_maintainerModifier(uint256 vaultId) public view { /* The modifier 'if (msg.sender != maintainer || !hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender))' should be 'if (msg.sender != maintainer && !hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender))' to match onlyVaultGoverner pattern */ }

invariants.description: During holding position removal via swap-and-pop, the isPositionUsed mapping is recalculated using calculatorConnector (struct field 0) instead of the ownerConnector (struct field 1, original msg.sender used to create the key). When calculatorConnector != ownerConnector, the position index mapping becomes corrupted, creating ghost entries and orphaned positions.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a position with onlyOwner=false is created by connector A but has calculatorConnector B, and another position is removed triggering a swap-and-pop that moves this position, the isPositionUsed mapping will store an incorrect key (using B instead of A). Future operations on this position will fail or produce incorrect indices. getHoldingPositionIndex returns 0 (not found) for the moved position, and a new holding position could be incorrectly created as a duplicate.
invariants.invariant_logic: For all holding positions at index i: isPositionUsed[keccak256(ownerConnector, positionId, data)] == i, where ownerConnector is holdingPositions[i] field 1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_positionIndexConsistency(uint256 vaultId) public view { HoldingPI[] memory positions = registry.getHoldingPositions(vaultId); for(uint i = 1; i < positions.length; i++) { bytes32 key = keccak256(abi.encode(positions[i].ownerConnector, positions[i].positionId, positions[i].data)); assert(registry.getHoldingPositionIndex(vaultId, positions[i].positionId, positions[i].ownerConnector, positions[i].data) == i); } }

invariants.description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function body, providing zero verification of withdrawal amounts. BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress relies on this function to validate that the accounting manager is not extracting more tokens than authorized during withdrawals.
invariants.attack_scenario: When the accountingManager calls sendTokensToTrustedAddress, it provides a newAmount via the data parameter. This newAmount is transferred without any validation since verifyRemoveLiquidity does nothing. A compromised or buggy accounting manager can extract arbitrary token amounts from connectors. The watcher security layer, designed as a safety net for monitoring suspicious activity, is completely absent.
invariants.invariant_logic: verifyRemoveLiquidity(withdrawAmount, sentAmount, data) SHOULD revert if sentAmount > withdrawAmount or if withdrawal violates protocol safety rules
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_watcherVerification() public view { /* Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity must contain non-trivial validation logic; empty body means no withdrawal safety check */ assert(false); }

invariants.description: The onlyExistingRoute modifier uses AND (&&) logic: it only reverts if route == address(0) AND isEnabled == false. A disabled route with a non-zero address passes the check. Since executeSwap and executeBridge never independently check isEnabled, disabled routes can still be executed.
invariants.attack_scenario: Maintainer or emergency role disables a malicious or compromised route via setEnableRoute(routeId, false). Attackers or keepers can still execute swaps/bridges through the disabled route because onlyExistingRoute doesn't properly gate on isEnabled. The emergency disable mechanism is ineffective.
invariants.invariant_logic: For any route used in executeSwap or executeBridge: routes[routeId].isEnabled == true AND routes[routeId].route != address(0)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_routeEnabled(uint256 routeId) public view { if (/* route is being used */) { assert(routes[routeId].isEnabled == true && routes[routeId].route != address(0)); } }

invariants.description: Multiple connector TVL calculations use unchecked subtraction (debt - collateral) that reverts on underflow in Solidity 0.8.x when a position is underwater. Since TVLHelper.getTVL iterates ALL positions, a single underwater position reverts the entire vault TVL calculation, potentially bricking deposits, withdrawals, and share price updates.
invariants.attack_scenario: Market conditions cause a lending position (Silo, Prisma, Compound, Dolomite) to become underwater (debt > collateral). The _getPositionTVL function reverts on the subtraction underflow. TVLHelper.getTVL reverts, and the accounting manager cannot calculate share prices. All vault operations that depend on TVL (deposits, withdrawals, fee calculations) are frozen indefinitely.
invariants.invariant_logic: _getPositionTVL MUST NOT revert even when totalDebt > totalCollateral; should return 0 instead of underflowing
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tvlNoRevert(uint256 vaultId) public view { /* TVLHelper.getTVL(vaultId, registry, baseToken) must never revert */ try TVLHelper.getTVL(vaultId, registry, baseToken) returns (uint256) {} catch { assert(false); } }

invariants.description: The _getPositionTVL function queries the Uniswap V3 pool's aggregate position using keccak256(abi.encodePacked(positionManager, tL, tU)) which returns the TOTAL liquidity for ALL NonfungiblePositionManager NFTs at those ticks, not just the specific tokenId. This inflates the TVL with liquidity from other users' positions at the same tick range.
invariants.attack_scenario: If other users have positions through the same NonfungiblePositionManager at the same tick range, the vault's TVL calculation includes their liquidity. This inflates the reported TVL, causing share price to be artificially high. New depositors receive fewer shares than deserved, and existing holders can withdraw more than their fair share.
invariants.invariant_logic: TVL for a specific tokenId MUST only reflect that NFT's liquidity, not aggregate pool position. Should use positionManager.positions(tokenId) to get per-NFT liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_uniTvlAccuracy(uint256 tokenId) public view { (uint128 nftLiquidity,,) = getCurrentLiquidity(tokenId); /* TVL should be calculated from nftLiquidity, not pool.positions(key).liquidity */ }

invariants.description: In receiveFlashLoan, the destinationConnector addresses used for arbitrary external calls are NOT validated as active connectors or trusted addresses. Only the receiver (where tokens are sent) is checked. The keeper can execute arbitrary calls to any contract through the flash loan mechanism.
invariants.attack_scenario: A compromised or malicious keeper uses the flash loan to call arbitrary contracts (e.g., approve tokens to external addresses, interact with unaudited protocols) through the destinationConnector array. Since only the token receiver is validated, the actual execution targets bypass all connector trust checks. This could drain connector funds or create unauthorized protocol interactions.
invariants.invariant_logic: For all i in destinationConnector: registry.isAnActiveConnector(vaultId, destinationConnector[i]) == true OR destinationConnector[i] == receiver
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_flashLoanTargets(uint256 vaultId, address[] memory destinationConnector) public view { for(uint i = 0; i < destinationConnector.length; i++) { assert(registry.isAddressTrusted(vaultId, destinationConnector[i])); } }

invariants.description: The flashLoan address stored in the Registry is global (not per-vault) and grants onlyManager access to ALL vaults through NoyaGovernanceBase.onlyManager modifier. Any connector's manager-restricted functions can be called by the flash loan contract regardless of which vault the connector belongs to.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the BalancerFlashLoan contract is compromised or its receiveFlashLoan caller check is bypassed, the attacker gains manager-level access to every connector in every vault. The single global flashLoan address creates a systemic risk across the entire protocol, as it's treated as equivalent to the keeper for all vaults.
invariants.invariant_logic: flashLoan access SHOULD be scoped per vault, OR the flashLoan contract MUST enforce per-vault authorization internally
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_flashLoanScope() public view { /* registry.flashLoan() is global; onlyManager checks msg.sender == registry.flashLoan() for all vaults */ }

invariants.description: getLatestUpdateTime returns the minimum positionTimestamp across all positions, but new holding positions are initialized with positionTimestamp = type(uint256).max. If no position has been explicitly timestamped via updateHoldingPostionWithTime, the function returns type(uint256).max, representing a far-future timestamp that defeats any staleness validation.
invariants.attack_scenario: Cross-chain TVL data becomes stale (e.g., LZ message fails), but same-chain positions have default type(uint256).max timestamps. getLatestUpdateTime returns type(uint256).max, and any freshness check comparing this to a threshold passes, allowing the accounting manager to use stale cross-chain TVL data for share price calculations. Depositors or withdrawers get incorrect share prices.
invariants.invariant_logic: getLatestUpdateTime(vaultId) <= block.timestamp for any active vault with positions
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_timestampFreshness(uint256 vaultId) public view { uint256 t = TVLHelper.getLatestUpdateTime(vaultId, registry); assert(t <= block.timestamp); }

invariants.description: The nonce in the Keepers contract must strictly increase on every successful execution to prevent replay attacks. The nonce is incremented before the external call, ensuring atomicity with the revert on failed calls.
invariants.invariant_logic: After each successful execute() call: nonce_after == nonce_before + 1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_nonceMonotonicity() public view { /* nonce is incremented exactly once per successful execute() call, old(nonce) < nonce after each call */ }

invariants.description: Bridge transaction approvals have no expiration after the BRIDGE_TXN_WAITING_TIME passes. Once approved and waiting time elapses, the bridge transaction can be executed at any point in the future, even if market conditions have changed significantly.
invariants.invariant_logic: approvedBridgeTXN[txn] should have both a minimum waiting time AND a maximum validity window: approvedBridgeTXN[txn] + BRIDGE_TXN_WAITING_TIME <= block.timestamp <= approvedBridgeTXN[txn] + MAX_VALIDITY
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_bridgeApprovalExpiry(bytes32 txn) public view { if(approvedBridgeTXN[txn] != 0) { assert(block.timestamp <= approvedBridgeTXN[txn] + MAX_VALIDITY_PERIOD); } }

invariants.description: The isEligibleToUse mapping can only be set to true via addEligibleUser but there is no function to remove eligible users. Once an address is granted swap/bridge access, it cannot be revoked even by the maintainer or emergency role.
invariants.invariant_logic: For every address with isEligibleToUse[addr] == true, there SHOULD exist a mechanism to set isEligibleToUse[addr] = false
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_eligibleUserRevocable(address user) public { /* No removeEligibleUser function exists; isEligibleToUse is irreversible */ }

invariants.description: repayWithCollateral function is missing the nonReentrant modifier, unlike all other state-changing functions in the connector. This creates a reentrancy surface if the Aave pool callback or token transfer has reentrant behavior.
invariants.invariant_logic: All state-changing external functions in connectors MUST have nonReentrant modifier
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reentrancyGuard() public { /* AaveConnector.repayWithCollateral is missing nonReentrant modifier */ }

invariants.description: transferPositionToAnotherConnector silently succeeds when the target connector is not active. The if-check means nothing happens when the connector is inactive, but the function doesn't revert, giving a false impression of successful execution.
invariants.invariant_logic: transferPositionToAnotherConnector MUST revert if !registry.isAnActiveConnector(vaultId, connector), not silently succeed
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_transferRequiresActiveConnector(address connector) public view { /* If connector is inactive, transferPositionToAnotherConnector should revert, not silently pass */ }

invariants.description: The holdingPositions array uses index 0 as a dummy/sentinel, and isPositionUsed returns 0 for non-existent positions. This creates an ambiguity: index 0 means both 'dummy position' and 'position not found'. If the dummy position at index 0 is somehow corrupted or matched, position lookups become unreliable. Additionally, removeTrustedPosition iterates all holdingPositions including the dummy, potentially blocking removal if positionId accidentally matches.
invariants.invariant_logic: holdingPositions[0].positionId == bytes32(0) AND holdingPositions[0].calculatorConnector == address(0) must always hold. isPositionUsed[key] == 0 implies no real position exists with that key.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_dummyPosition(uint256 vaultId) public view { HoldingPI memory p = registry.getHoldingPosition(vaultId, 0); assert(p.calculatorConnector == address(0) && p.positionId == bytes32(0)); }
